purpose: Template for documenting why an external robot or simulator is credible enough to validate Paper 119 without relying on Haonan Chen or any target collaborator.

task_fidelity.contact_or_dynamics_property: insertion contact and regrasp basin transition
task_fidelity.minimum_required_signal: peg/object pose plus contact or force proxy

task_fidelity.contact_or_dynamics_property: drawer joint/contact and reachable grasp basin
task_fidelity.minimum_required_signal: drawer joint state, object pose, and contact or force proxy

task_fidelity.contact_or_dynamics_property: door hinge/contact transition into navigation start set
task_fidelity.minimum_required_signal: door state, base pose, clearance, and contact or force proxy
endpoint pose, cable state proxy, and contact or force proxy

acceptance_gates.name: platform_provenance_complete
acceptance_gates.evidence: platform name, version, physics/contact details, robot/assets, sensors, and timestep are filled before rollouts

acceptance_gates.name: paired_reset_replay_verified
acceptance_gates.evidence: the same scene, seed, skill pair, and initial-state hash can be replayed for every method

acceptance_gates.name: contact_failure_observable
acceptance_gates.evidence: each task logs state plus contact/force proxy and records videos for successes, failures, repairs, and abstentions

acceptance_gates.name: non_oracle_methods_fair
acceptance_gates.evidence: all non-oracle methods share skills, observations, reset IDs, and compute budget, with no hidden future state

acceptance_gates.name: raw_logs_drive_metrics
acceptance_gates.evidence: manifest metrics are recomputed from JSONL logs by validate_external_rollouts.py
